entries.title: Hotfix — Falling Through the World
notes: Fixed players (especially new ones) falling through the floor and blacking out when moving into areas that were still loading in. The ground now holds you steady while the terrain streams.

entries.title: Leveling & Loot
notes: Leveling up now actually makes you stronger — each level gives a clear, noticeable jump in your damage (it barely moved before).
Eased the early XP pace a little so the first few levels aren't over in a handful of kills.
That rare food drop is now FAR rarer — a true once-in-a-blue-moon find worth bragging about.
Loot pickups are more reliable, and dropped loot bags now tidy themselves up after a few minutes.

entries.title: Hotfix — Camera & Polish
notes: Fixed a camera glitch where walking up close to another player could black out most of your screen.
Reworked the class descriptions on the character creation screen.
Stability: the menus can no longer get stuck during a screen transition.

entries.title: Enemies, Levels & Loot
notes: The world fights back — goblins now roam the Young Wood. They spot you, give chase, trade blows, and respawn over time. The Training Grounds stay a safe haven.
Earn XP and level up: every kill grants experience as you climb from level 1 toward 50, growing steadily stronger. A gold XP bar and your level sit on the HUD, with a flourish each time you level.
Falling in battle now has weight — a proper death screen, then a respawn back at the Training Grounds with a small XP setback. Duel-pit sparring stays penalty-free.

Real gear, finally yours — press I for your bag and C for the new Character screen: a rotatable 3D model of your hero wearing full paper-doll equipment (head, shoulders, chest, gloves, legs, boots, weapon, offhand) beside a complete stats sheet. Drag items between your bag and your gear.
Loot drops — defeated goblins can leave a treasure chest behind. Walk up, press F, and take what's inside (Loot All, or drag and Shift-click items into your bag).
Food you can eat — right-click a consumable in your bag to eat it and heal up. And keep your eyes open... something very rare and very delicious is out there.
Pick up where you left off — you log back in right where you logged out, with your custom hotbar intact.
New Graphics settings — anti-aliasing, shadow quality and more, so the game runs and looks its best on your machine.
Loads of combat and interface polish — clearer hit numbers and a satisfying camera punch, smarter auto-attack, and nameplates and health bars that no longer show through walls.

entries.title: Class Identity, Chat & Bug Reports
notes: Every class now plays to its own identity — distinct health and mana pools, and a real combat system underneath it all: critical hits, dodges, armor, and physical vs. Aetheric damage.

Core abilities (press E): each class has a signature, innate power. Arcanist — Blink (teleport); Daring — Stealth (vanish until an enemy closes in); Sentinel — Fortify (toggle on innate damage resistance); Warden — Restoration (a channeled heal).
Arcanist — Arcane Burst: aim a ring on the ground and detonate an Aetheric blast on your target.
Daring — Flicker Strike: flick behind your target and strike from blind with a big bonus to crit.
Sentinel — Shield Charge: hold to wind up the distance, release to physically rush your foe with your shield raised (needs a shield equipped).

Warden — reborn as a robed, staff-wielding healer: cast Aether Mend on yourself or an ally, Renewing Pulse to heal everyone nearby (it chains friend to friend), and fling the Aether Lance as a ranged attack.

Combat feel: abilities require the right weapon in hand, a clear cast bar shows what you're casting, hotbar slots light up only when usable, and damage + heal numbers float right at whoever they land on.
In-world chat — press Enter to talk to everyone in the realm.
Report bugs without leaving the game: type /bug for a form with a subject, description, and up to 5 screenshots (it can even snapshot your current screen). Reports come straight to the dev.
A full Settings menu — rebind any key, including your hotbar slots, and adjust mouse look sensitivity and invert-Y. Open it from the title-screen cog or the pause menu.

The Training Grounds were rebuilt: spawn centered in a clearing lit by floating Aether-crystal lamps, then walk the avenue past the Armory to a Target Range and a Heal Yard (with wounded-ally dummies to practice healing on), ending at a big Duel Pit.
Snappier jump and tuned movement speeds (walk, combat-trot, and sprint).

entries.title: Stability
notes: Fixed a bug where you could briefly fall through the world right after joining.

entries.title: The Armoury
notes: The weapon selection got a proper home — a wooden armoury rack in the Training Grounds courtyard, with each class's weapon resting above its banner.
Moved it off the spawn pad and out of the path, opening the courtyard up toward the practice dummies.

entries.title: Combat & Weapons
notes: New — the Arcanist is now a ranged caster: wield the Aether Wand and fire traveling magic bolts that can miss, be dodged, and are blocked by walls.
You can now see other players blocking — the guard pose holds even while moving.
The Training Grounds are solid now — walls, towers, and weapon pedestals no longer let you walk through them.
Stronger jump — you can finally clear the pit walls.
Per-weapon swing animations: sword, warhammer, and dagger each swing their own way, and swings were slowed down so they actually read.
Every weapon now sits properly in-hand (sword, shield, scythe, hammer, dagger).
Interact is now F (was E), and the interact prompt is centered on screen.
The spellbook closes with Esc, and the camera no longer fights you while it's open.
Fixed a camera snap when holding both mouse buttons and releasing one.

entries.title: Starter Weapons
notes: Each class can grab its starter weapon from the rack in the Training Grounds and wield it — visible to everyone in multiplayer.
Armed swing animations play when you attack with a weapon.
Press U anywhere to stow your weapon back to fists.

entries.title: Online Play & First Playtest
notes: A dedicated, always-on server so friends can meet in the same world.
First-playtest fixes: training-dummy damage, respawn if you fall off the world, the Esc pause menu, and smoother blocking.
Fixed a logging flood that could crash a client during long sessions.

entries.title: First Playable Realm
notes: The first online build: Title → Character Creation → a shared 3D world.
Create a Woken from three peoples and four classes and walk the Young Wood together.
Third-person MMO camera, nameplates, and live multiplayer movement.
This launcher — download once, stay updated, click Play.
